notes_md:
**mồ hôi** = sweat (the liquid your body makes when you’re hot, nervous, or exercising).

It’s an uncountable noun, but you can say **giọt mồ hôi** (a drop of sweat) to count it. Very common verbs are **ra mồ hôi** and **đổ mồ hôi** (to sweat), and you’ll hear body parts like **mồ hôi tay** (sweaty hands).  


Metaphorically, **mồ hôi** can also suggest hard work and effort, like “blood and sweat” in English.
exampleSentences: Sau khi chạy bộ, anh thấy mồ hôi chảy rất nhiều trên trán.
Em luôn lau mồ hôi khi tập thể dục ở phòng gym.
Chị cảm thấy mồ hôi ướt áo khi trời nắng nóng.
exampleSentencesTranslated: After jogging, he sees sweat running a lot on his forehead.
I always wipe my sweat when exercising at the gym.
She feels sweat soaking her shirt when the weather is hot.
notes:

Metaphorically, mồ hôi can also suggest hard work and effort, like “blood and sweat” in English.
